text.title: Index Tuning — Failure Modes
text.summary: Five antipatterns in HNSW + quantization tuning.

failure-mode.description: Tune without bench — knobs changed based on "feels faster"; no measurement; sometimes worse than defaults.
failure-mode.detector: commit changing hnsw.* without paired bench output
failure-mode.repair: Run baseline + tuned bench; commit both together.

failure-mode.description: Aggressive quantization (binary) breaks recall — 32× compression but recall drops to 0.65.
failure-mode.repair: Step down to product quantization (8×); if still below floor, scalar int8.

failure-mode.description: ef_search left at default (16) in production — latency low but recall mediocre on hard queries.
failure-mode.detector: hnsw.ef_search==16 AND production workload is read-heavy
failure-mode.repair: Tune ef_search by sweep: 32, 64, 128. Pick best recall under latency cap.

failure-mode.description: No tuning-history record — team re-debates tuning every quarter; institutional memory lost.
failure-mode.detector: tuning_history_path missing OR file does not exist
failure-mode.repair: Initialise tuning-history.yaml. Append every iteration with date + params + bench.

failure-mode.description: Memory cap ignored — M=64 sounded fast in bench; production OOM kills DB.
failure-mode.repair: Reduce M OR enable quantization. Never exceed memory cap in production.
